mechanism_narrative: Persistent high inflation, as measured by CPIAUCSL, erodes corporate profit margins and consumer purchasing power, leading to a general decline in equity valuations and negative S&P 500 forward returns as investors anticipate tighter monetary policy.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in more than 60% of periods where CPIAUCSL is above its 12-month moving average, or the Rank IC is consistently negative.

mechanism_narrative: A significant and rapid increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals a deteriorating labor market, which directly impacts consumer confidence and spending, leading to reduced corporate earnings and negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in more than 50% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the signal fails to predict downturns.

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales (RSAFS) show sustained strength while the Federal Funds Rate (FEDFUNDS) is already elevated, it indicates an overheating economy that will likely prompt further monetary tightening, increasing discount rates and negatively impacting equity valuations.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in more than 60% of triggered instances, or the interaction term shows no significant predictive power.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, a high Federal Funds Rate (FEDFUNDS) increases borrowing costs for businesses and consumers, dampening economic activity and reducing corporate profitability, which leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in more than 50% of triggered instances when FEDFUNDS is above 4.5%, or the correlation with returns is positive.

mechanism_narrative: A significant rebound in housing starts (HOUST) after a period of decline signals renewed consumer and business confidence, stimulating construction activity and related sectors, which contributes to overall economic growth and positive S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises > 10% in 3 months after a 6-month decline
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in more than 50% of triggered instances despite the positive signal, or the recovery in HOUST does not precede market gains.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS) indicates a robust labor market, supporting strong consumer spending and corporate revenues, which translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over short horizons.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns are not significantly positive during periods of PAYEMS growth.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained increases in industrial production (INDPRO) reflect expanding manufacturing and supply chain activity, indicating healthy corporate output and potentially higher earnings, which supports positive S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO growth > 0.5% month-over-month for 3 consecutive months
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in more than 50% of triggered instances despite the positive signal, or INDPRO growth does not correlate with market gains.

mechanism_narrative: High and persistent inflation (CPIAUCSL) combined with strong retail sales (RSAFS) signals an economy that is overheating, compelling the central bank to maintain or increase restrictive monetary policy (FEDFUNDS), which negatively impacts equity markets by raising discount rates and reducing liquidity.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL > 300 AND RSAFS growth > 0.5% month-over-month AND FEDFUNDS > 5.0%
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in more than 40% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the combined effect of these proxies is not significant.
